title: 10 medicinal constituents of Santalum album (sandalwood)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Santalum album sesquiterpenes is a class of natural compounds responsible for its distinctive aroma and therapeutic properties. These volatile molecules are found in the heartwood of the tree and are released during distillation, giving sandalwood essential oil its characteristic woody, sweet scent. Sesquiterpenes have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making them valuable for various applications in perfumery, cosmetics, and traditional medicine.

Santalum album α-santalol is a natural compound derived from the heartwood of the sandalwood tree. It is a sesquiterpene, a type of essential oil that is responsible for the distinctive fragrance and aroma of sandalwood. α-Santalol has been used in perfumery, cosmetics, and traditional medicine for centuries due to its unique properties, including its calming and sedative effects. It is also valued for its ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels.

Santalum album β-santalol is a natural sesquiterpene compound that is extracted from the heartwood of this highly prized tree. It is responsible for the characteristic fragrance and aroma of sandalwood oil, which has been used in traditional medicine, perfumery, and incense production for centuries. β-Santalol is also known for its therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.

Santalum album epoxysantalone A is a natural sesquiterpene compound extracted from the heartwood of Sandalwood trees (Santalum album). This precious oil is highly valued for its distinct, woody aroma and is widely used in perfumery, cosmetics, and traditional medicine. Epoxysantalone A is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in various applications. Its unique fragrance profile has also made it a staple in the production of high-end fragrances.

Santalum album santalbic acid is a type of terpenoid compound that is found in the heartwood of the sandalwood tree. It is responsible for the distinctive fragrance and aroma of high-quality sandalwood oil. Santalbic acid has been used in perfumery, aromatherapy, and traditional medicine for centuries due to its unique scent and potential therapeutic properties. It is also used as a natural preservative and fixative in fragrances.

Santalum album triterpenes is a class of bioactive compounds found in the oil extracted from the heartwood of this sacred tree. These triterpenes have been extensively studied for their potential therapeutic properties, including antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory activities. They are also responsible for the distinctive aroma and fragrance of sandalwood essential oil, which is highly prized for its use in perfumery, cosmetics, and traditional medicine.

Santalum album germacrane D is a unique compound found in the essential oil of Santalum album. It belongs to the class of sesquiterpenoids and is responsible for the distinctive woody, earthy aroma of sandalwood. Germacrane D has been reported to have several biological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable ingredient in traditional medicine and perfumery applications.

Santalum album β-eudesmol is a sesquiterpenoid compound naturally occurring in the heartwood of this esteemed tree species. This fragrant molecule is responsible for the distinctive aroma and medicinal properties of sandalwood oil, highly valued for its use in perfumes, incense, and traditional medicine. β-Eudesmol has also been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it a sought-after ingredient in various industries.

Santalum album α-eudesmol is a sesquiterpene compound found in the heartwood of this highly valued tree. It is a major component of the essential oil extracted from the wood, responsible for its distinctive sweet, woody fragrance and aroma. α-Eudesmol has been traditionally used in perfumery, aromatherapy, and traditional medicine due to its calming and antiseptic properties. Its unique scent and therapeutic benefits make it highly sought after in the industry.

Santalum album epoxysantalone B is a bioactive sesquiterpene lactone derived from the roots of this plant. It has been reported to possess various pharmacological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Epoxysantalone B has also shown cytotoxic effects against several human cancer cell lines, making it a potential lead compound for the development of new anticancer drugs. Further research is needed to fully understand its mechanisms of action and therapeutic potential.

intro: Santalum album has active constituents such as beta-santene and alpha-santalol. These sesquiterpenes possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The constituents of sandalwood can improve people's lives by providing relief from skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues, while also promoting relaxation, reducing stress, and improving sleep quality.
intro_desc: Santalum album has active constituents such as alpha-santalol, beta-santalol, and santalol. These compounds possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. They can also improve respiratory health by reducing inflammation and opening airways, providing relief for individuals with asthma and other respiratory conditions. This can significantly improve the quality of life for those affected, promoting better breathing and overall well-being.
